title: Projects & Case Studies
text:
Selected projects that demonstrate my financial analysis, capital budgeting, and investment skills.


During my Corporate Finance course at University Canada West, I completed a full capital budgeting evaluation for a hypothetical Canadian manufacturing firm’s expansion project.

Methods: NPV, IRR, Payback Period, and sensitivity analysis on discount rate and sales growth.

Tools: Advanced Excel financial modeling.

Outcome: Recommended approval — positive NPV of ~CAD 52,000 and IRR of 18% (exceeding cost of capital).

This directly applied the capital budgeting techniques highlighted in my bio.



Building on my role as Financial Analyst, I led an internal project to improve cost tracking and reporting.

Methods: Variance analysis, trend analysis, ratio analysis on income statements and balance sheets.

Tools: Advanced Excel dashboards.

Outcome: Identified inefficiencies that increased profits by 10% and improved working capital management.




In Investment Analysis, I constructed and evaluated a diversified portfolio targeted at the Canadian market.

Methods: Ratio & trend analysis, cash flow evaluation, sector allocation for risk management.

Tools: Excel + introductory SQL for data handling.

Outcome: Portfolio with projected 7–9% annual return while maintaining balanced risk exposure (finance & energy sectors).

These projects showcase my readiness for banking and investment roles in Canada.
